reason: The text explicitly presents Jonah proclaiming an upcoming supernatural destruction of Nineveh by Yahweh: 'Yet forty days, and Nineveh shall be overthrown' (וְנִֽינְוֵ֖ה נֶהְפָּֽכֶת). This destruction is clearly not individual but affects the entire city of Nineveh (described as a 'great city'—הָעִ֣יר הַגְּדוֹלָ֑ה) and its population. God's subsequent statement emphasizes the scale by referencing 'more than 120,000 persons' in the city who would be affected. The text demonstrates that this is a public, city-wide calamity threatened to befall the general population, meeting both criteria of the statement.
